title: After Rs 3.3 lakh crore infra push, Airtel eyes cloud, NBFC and data centres: Sunil Mittal
description: The strategy marks a shift beyond connectivity as Airtel looks to monetise India's accelerating adoption of artificial intelligence (AI), cloud computing and digital infrastructure while leveraging its nationwide telecom network.

title: Trump administration targets state AI laws over ideology
description: The Trump administration is continuing its pushback against state artificial intelligence laws that it views as ideologically biased, proposing a new Federal Trade Commission policy. The proposed policy statement, which is open for public comment through July 31, would affect how the FTC regulates AI companies. The agency said it’s meant to address concerns that “AI companies that distort ...

title: The growing problem of ‘skill fishing’ in the hiring world
description: As AI and skills-based hiring reshape recruitment, some candidates are overstating their proficiency in sought-after skills such as artificial intelligence, coding and data analysis, a practice experts call ‘skill fishing’

title: From space rovers to hunger maps: How AI is reshaping humanitarian aid
description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is often discussed for its potential threats to humanity. But humanitarian organisations are using it to predict hunger, map destruction and deliver aid without sending people into danger.
content:
Delivering food through conflict zones, minefields and floods can put humanitarian workers at mortal risk.
Now, technology developed to control rovers on distant planets is being adapted to take aid workers out of some of the world’s most dangerous a... [3746 chars]

title: AI, science and the risks in China’s reliance on imported precision equipment
description: The country is dependent on overseas high-end scientific instruments, crimping use of artificial intelligence, researcher says
content:
China’s reliance on imports for the most sophisticated scientific instruments could hold back the country’s use of AI in science, according to a leading Chinese researcher.

title: Simaero plans to add more simulators, tap local engg talent in India: CEO
description: French firm Simaero will invest 200 million euros in India over ten years. The company plans to expand its aviation training facility in Gurugram. It aims to develop local engineering talent for simulator technology services. Simaero expects to operate four to six simulators by 2029. Artificial intelligence will enhance operational efficiency and learning experiences.
content:
